summary: Revision preserves ADR + impacted-domain coherence. (1) workflow-only is now CORRECT and COMPLETE. Verified the actual branch diff (git diff --name-only main...HEAD): only .mindspec/docs/specs/100-.../spec.md and .beads/issues.jsonl change today; no internal/contextpack file is touched, and the R1 changed-file set (normalization helper + divergence.go/plan.go/ownership.go under internal/validate, internal/adr parse.go test, cmd/mindspec/adr.go, internal/approve/spec.go, ADR-0032 doc) all map to workflow's OWNERSHIP.yaml globs. workflow claims internal/validate/**, internal/ownership/**, internal/adr/**, internal/approve/**, cmd/**. helper_location_owned: BOTH candidate landing sites are workflow-owned — internal/validate/** (line 7) and internal/ownership/** (line 10); internal/ownership exists as a real package (populate.go/source.go/stub.go) so the helper genuinely can land there, and either way it's workflow. Confirmed exclusivity: grepped all four domain manifests (context-system, core, execution, workflow) — NO other domain claims any of the five impacted globs, so no leak into core/execution/context-system. context-system correctly DROPPED: its only justification was the now-removed contextpack/spec.go edit, and ParseSpec staying dumb is consistent across Goal/Background/Scope/Non-Goals. The ADR-0032 doc edit lands under .mindspec/docs/adr/** which isDocFile->isProcessArtifact skips at the divergence gate (verified divergence.go:260 + docsync.go:330), so it needs no domain claim — spec's claim accurate. (2) ADR coverage with workflow-only impacted domain is clean: ADR-0036 (Accepted; Domains workflow,validation,doc-sync,ownership) AND ADR-0024 (Accepted; adr,context-system,workflow) BOTH declare workflow; coveredAccepted holds. (3) Self-consistency: every spec-100 changed file resolves to workflow, workflow is covered by cited Accepted ADRs, ADR-0032 doc is gate-skipped — no self-block at complete. (4) ADR-0032-as-amended is accurate: ADR-0032 sub-decision 1 (lines 43-50) literally REJECTS path-like identifiers as ambiguous; R1's normalize-at-source softens exactly that by RESOLVING a path-like Impacted-Domains entry to its unique owning dir-name (erroring on 0/>1 owners), which is a genuine amendment of sub-decision 1, mirroring the 099->ADR-0037 amendment pattern. Characterization matches the ADR's actual text; ADR-0032 stays Accepted so coverage remains valid. No coherence regression from round 1.
